title: Singapore to Paris via Doha
summary: A business-class fare that behaves like premium economy pricing if you can tolerate an overnight transit.
catchSummary: One overnight layover in Doha with a later arrival than the direct options.
whyWorthIt: You are paying roughly forty-two percent of the usual premium fare for a top-tier business-class product.

title: Kuala Lumpur to Tokyo with JAL First
summary: A rare first-class fare that only really makes sense if you are happy to reposition into Kuala Lumpur.
catchSummary: You need a separate positioning flight into Kuala Lumpur to unlock the fare.
whyWorthIt: The fare lands unusually close to standard long-haul business-class pricing while delivering a true first-class cabin.

title: Sydney to Rome via Southeast Asia and Istanbul
summary: A weird routing with two stops, but the price compression makes the pain surprisingly rational.
catchSummary: Two stops and a noticeably longer journey than the cleaner premium options.
whyWorthIt: If you optimize for value over convenience, this is still far cheaper than most premium fares into Southern Europe.

title: Bangkok Riverside Suite Soft-Opening Rate
summary: A five-star riverside stay with soft-opening pricing and weekday stackable perks.
catchSummary: Best pricing only holds on weekday check-ins and shorter stays.
whyWorthIt: The effective rate drops meaningfully below normal luxury-stay pricing once the opening promo and included credits are stacked.
